name: WebBL Connection Pool Shutdown - Deep Analysis
description: Deep analysis of connection pool shutdown issue in webbl module. Answering 10 specific questions from prompt file, checking production logs, comparing with other modules, and updating investigation document.

entries.summary: ROOT CAUSE FOUND: InttraDBListener loads 1.87M records → OutOfMemoryError → connection pool destroyed
entries.detail:
Exact sequence on task 4ad01fb7:
1. 15:29:20.378 — InttraDBListener: Number of Web BL found for archiving to S3 :1867870 (1.87M records loaded!)
2. 15:29:51.336 — java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space (384m heap exhausted)
3. 15:29:51.337 — SQSListener: OutOfMemoryError propagated
4. 15:29:51.340 — Connection pool shut down (OOM-triggered GC destroys Apache HTTP pool)

90-day frequency: millions of errors per day (10.3M on Mar 31 alone). 9 different tasks affected in 7 days. Tight-loop retry with no backoff generates massive log volume.

AWS SDK v2 version: 2.30.24

entries.summary: Full chain traced: MyBatis query has NO LIMIT, loads 1.87M records. OOM confirmed across all tasks. Other modules compared.
entries.detail:
InttraDBListener chain: WebBLMapper.getWebBLProcessRequests() runs SELECT with NO LIMIT — loads ALL 'SUBMITTED' records. 1.87M records on 384m heap → OOM → pool destroyed.

Pattern confirmed across ALL tasks:
- task 25818ea1: 1,870,385 records → OOM at 20:04:54
- task 4ad01fb7: 1,870,259 records → OOM at 19:52, again 1,867,870 → OOM at 15:29
- task 25818ea1: 1,869,817 records → OOM at 18:59

Double-start causes duplicate scheduled runs every 30 min.

Modules comparison: booking & booking-bridge have same double-start. visibility does NOT.

AWS SDK v2 version: 2.30.24, apache-client included.

entries.summary: Updated investigation document with all 10 answers. Session complete.
entries.detail:
Updated webbl/docs/2026-03-31-sqs-connection-pool-shutdown-investigation.md with complete analysis answering all 10 questions from the prompt file.

Key findings:
1. ROOT CAUSE: InttraDBListener loads 1.87M records via WebBLMapper.getWebBLProcessRequests() (NO LIMIT clause) → OOM on 384m heap → JVM finalization destroys Apache HTTP connection pool
2. FREQUENCY: 35M+ errors in 7 days across 9 tasks. 10M+ errors/day on some days
3. IMPACT: Tasks become zombies — health check passes but SQS processing is dead
4. No explicit close() exists anywhere — pool shutdown is caused by JVM finalization during OOM
5. booking and booking-bridge have same double-start bug; visibility does NOT
6. Duplicate submit() queues backup listener; duplicate scheduleWithFixedDelay creates independent schedules
7. DBConnections = NetworkDBListener (retry records) + InttraDBListener (1.87M archive records)
8. JVM_Xmx=384m > ECS memory=256 MiB — fundamentally broken sizing
9. Need permanent failure detection + backoff in SQSListener
10. Need AutoCloseable on MessagingClient + awaitTermination in ListenerManager.stop()

Model: Claude Opus 4.6

entries.summary: Definitive timeline: OOM from ION-12446 (2025-06), pool shutdown from ION-14546 AWS upgrade (deployed Mar 14). Externally-managed HTTP client correction.
entries.detail:
DEFINITIVE TIMELINE:
- 2025-06-03: Sumesh Jacob (ION-12446) introduces InttraDBListener + unbounded query. NOT the AWS upgrade.
- 2026-03-03/04: Arijit Kundu (ION-14546) commits WebBL AWS SDK v2 upgrade
- 2026-03-14: AWS upgrade deployed to prod. FIRST pool shutdown errors: 3,421,520

Before Mar 14: 0 pool shutdown, 10-69 OOM/day
After Mar 14: millions pool shutdown/day, 4-35 OOM/day

The OOM was always there. The AWS upgrade (Apache HTTP client) made OOM CATASTROPHIC because PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager.shutdown() is permanent + tight-loop retry.

CRITICAL CORRECTION: .httpClient(createHttpClient()) makes HTTP client externally managed → SqsClient.close() will NOT close it. AutoCloseable on MessagingClient alone won't fix it for graceful shutdown. Must either change to .httpClientBuilder() or keep separate reference to ApacheHttpClient.

entries.summary: Document updated with git history, pre/post comparison, and externally-managed HTTP client correction
entries.detail:
Added 3 new sections to investigation document:

§11. Git History — Sumesh Jacob introduced unbounded query in ION-12446 (2025-06-03), NOT the AWS upgrade
§12. Pre/Post AWS Upgrade comparison — ZERO pool shutdown before Mar 14, 3.4M on Mar 14 (deployment day), millions daily after
§13. Externally-Managed HTTP Client analysis — AutoCloseable alone won't help because .httpClient() makes SDK NOT close it. Must change to .httpClientBuilder() or keep separate ApacheHttpClient reference. For OOM root cause, AutoCloseable is irrelevant — the fix is LIMIT clause.

Model: Claude Opus 4.6

entries.summary: Completed §14 SDK 1.x vs 2.x resilience analysis and §15 cloud-sdk-aws refactoring recommendations
entries.detail:
Key findings:

SDK 1.x vs 2.x:
- Decompiled apache-client-2.42.23.jar: NO Cleaner, finalize, PhantomReference, or ShutdownHook found. Pool shutdown is NOT caused by GC finalization.
- SDK 1.x: AmazonSQSClientBuilder.defaultClient() creates internally-managed HTTP client. Pool survives OOM. Task eventually crashes → ECS restarts → fresh pool.
- SDK 2.x: ApacheHttpClient created externally, passed via .httpClient(). Pool gets permanently destroyed within 4ms of OOM. Task stays alive as zombie.
- The irony: SDK 2.x's pool destruction PREVENTS the task from crashing (no memory pressure from fast-failing ISE), keeping the zombie alive.
- Pre-Mar-14 CloudWatch: OOM at 17:03:17 → task restart at 17:05:01, ZERO pool-shutdown errors.

Cloud-sdk-aws refactoring (7 issues identified):
1. SqsMessagingClient not AutoCloseable
2. MessagingClient interface missing close() contract  
3. Factory uses .httpClient() (externally managed) — reference LOST, cannot be closed
4. handleException() doesn't classify permanent failures (pool shutdown)
5. SnsService not AutoCloseable
6. HTTP client config not configurable
7. Listener interface missing AutoCloseable

Recommended: Phase 1 in commons 1.0.23-SNAPSHOT (change to .httpClientBuilder(), add AutoCloseable, add ConnectionPoolShutdownException). Phase 2 in webbl/booking-bridge (lifecycle management, backoff, double-start fix).

Document updated: webbl/docs/2026-03-31-sqs-connection-pool-shutdown-investigation.md now has 16 sections.

entries.summary: ECS memory metrics prove spikes existed before Mar 14 — SDK 2.x made them SUSTAINED (no more restarts)
entries.detail:
ECS Memory Metrics (hourly, Mar 13-14):
- Before deployment: Avg=184-190%, Max=240-244%, Min periodically drops to 29-94% (TASK RESTARTS from OOM crashes)
- Mar 14 at 05:00: Min=94.1% (last restart before deployment)
- Mar 14 at 10:00: Min=26.7% (DEPLOYMENT - new task definition rolled out)
- Mar 14 after 10:00: Min NEVER drops below 159% again. Avg climbs steadily 176→187→193→195→200→201%
- After deployment, NO MORE low-Min-value hours = tasks stopped crashing

InttraDBListener Record Count Growth (weekly, Jan-Mar 2026):
- Jan 1: 998K-1.05M records, 403 executions/week
- Feb 1: 1.28M-1.36M, 387 exec/week
- Mar 1: 1.57M-1.62M, 260 exec/week
- Mar 8: 1.62M-1.70M, 247 exec/week
- Mar 15 (post-deploy): 1.70M-1.76M, **34 exec/week** (MASSIVE drop!)
- Mar 29: 1.84M-1.87M, 37 exec/week

KEY INSIGHT: Record count grew from 1M to 1.87M over 3 months. The Max memory spike was ALWAYS ~245%. What changed on Mar 14 is that tasks stopped crashing and restarting (zombie behavior), making the spike SUSTAINED instead of transient sawtooth.
